title: Emergency management — status epilepticus
candidateInstructions: You are an FY2 in the ED resuscitation bay. Mr Marcus Jenkins, a 35-year-old known epileptic, was found by his partner having a generalised tonic-clonic seizure 25 minutes ago. He is still seizing. IV access is in place. Please manage this emergency.
examinerNotes: Status epilepticus (>5 min or recurrent without recovery). Treatment ladder: IV lorazepam 4mg → repeat → phenytoin/levetiracetam/valproate → general anaesthesia. ABCDEs first. Check glucose immediately. Call anaesthetics early. Critical error: not giving benzodiazepine as first-line.

patient.name: Marcus Jenkins
patient.presentingComplaint: Ongoing generalised tonic-clonic seizure for 25 minutes
patient.reasonForAttendance: Partner called 999 when seizure did not stop

examination.general: Ongoing tonic-clonic movement, cyanotic, foaming, tongue biting
examination.neurological: GCS 3, still seizing, pupils equal and reactive

investigations.name: Point-of-care blood glucose
investigations.interpretation: Normal blood glucose — hypoglycaemia is NOT the cause of this seizure.

patient.emotionalState: Unconscious — no meaningful response possible during seizure
patient.communicationStyle: Cannot communicate — obtunded. Information from partner.

primaryDiagnosis: Status Epilepticus — precipitated by anti-epileptic drug withdrawal

dataGathering.detailedExpectation: Seizure >5 minutes or failure to recover between seizures = status epilepticus
Blood glucose is immediate investigation to exclude hypoglycaemia

clinicalManagement.detailedExpectation: High-flow oxygen, recovery position if not actively seizing, airway adjuncts

clinicalManagement.criterion: IV lorazepam 4mg as first-line (or IV diazepam)
clinicalManagement.detailedExpectation: Benzodiazepine first-line: IV lorazepam 4mg (preferred) or IV diazepam 10mg

clinicalManagement.criterion: Second-line antiepileptic (phenytoin, levetiracetam, valproate) if no response
clinicalManagement.detailedExpectation: IV phenytoin 20mg/kg or levetiracetam 60mg/kg or sodium valproate 40mg/kg

clinicalManagement.criterion: Calls anaesthetics early for intubation if not responding
clinicalManagement.detailedExpectation: RSI/general anaesthesia for refractory status epilepticus

interpersonalSkills.detailedExpectation: Brief explanation to partner about what is happening

references.source: NICE CG137: Epilepsies — status epilepticus management
Convulsive Status Epilepticus treatment algorithm NICE 2022

learningObjectives: Check blood glucose in any seizure
